skill.name: lobster-cognitive-growth
skill.description: Train an AI lobster or agent with cognitive drills, learning journals, reasoning checks, memory review, and owner feedback loops.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents through repeatable cognitive growth drills and reflection routines.
skill.author: norika1207-lab

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, reconnaissance, and weak-cryptography patterns, but most command and cryptography hits are Markdown false positives. The confirmed concerns are an optional authenticated Charenix integration, an unpinned remote curl install command, and external links that require user trust; no confirmed malicious intent or automatic exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated External Persistence Integration
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Charenix API endpoints as a persistence layer and instructs users to send X-Agent-Key on protected requests. This is a legitimate integration pattern, but journals, history, strategies, and an agent key may leave the local environment if users enable it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint list, base URL, and authentication header are explicit. I did not find evidence that the skill automatically sends data, so the concern is controlled external data sharing rather than confirmed exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned Remote Install Command
medium_findings.description: The README recommends a curl command that downloads SKILL.md from a branch URL into the local skills directory. This is common for installation docs, but the target can change over time because it is not pinned to an immutable commit.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell command and remote URL are directly present in the README. The risk depends on a user manually running the command, and no auto-execution code was found.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Most Ruby or shell backtick detections occur inside fenced text, HTTP, JSON, or prompt examples. They are documentation examples, not executable code paths inside this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced Markdown examples and contain no runnable script files or invocation mechanism. This strongly supports dismissing the bulk of the command-execution detections as false positives.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links Require User Trust
low_findings.description: The skill and README include external links to Charenix, GitHub, ClawHub, and AgentSkill. These are documentation and listing links, not automatic network requests, but users should verify the destinations before using integrations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plainly visible and are not wrapped in executable code at these locations. The residual risk is user navigation or optional integration, not hidden network activity.

low_findings.title: Agent-Facing Owner Prompt Should Remain Untrusted Content
low_findings.description: The skill includes an owner prompt that tells the agent it is allowed to use the skill and sets goals for behavior. I did not find instructions to ignore higher-priority rules, skip review, or override system policy, so this is not confirmed prompt injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The block is clearly a prompt template and uses agent-directed wording. It lacks the stronger override language that would justify a high or critical prompt-injection finding.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections map to prose describing intelligence and cognition, not to MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic use. No cryptographic operation was found in the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain descriptive text about agent training. There is no code, algorithm name, digest call, or security-sensitive cryptographic context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Manual Remote Download Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The install example uses curl to download a skill file from a mutable branch URL. Users should inspect the downloaded file or pin a trusted commit before installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is present in a bash block and targets a remote raw GitHub URL. The command is user-run documentation, not hidden execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Optional Authenticated API Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents GET and POST endpoints for agent history, journals, hypotheses, and strategy, plus an X-Agent-Key header. This can expose sensitive agent state if used without informed consent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API paths, header, and base URL are explicit in the integration section. There is no evidence of automatic requests in the skill files.

content.user_title: Train AI Agents with Cognitive Growth Drills
content.value_statement: AI agents can repeat habits without improving from feedback. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured drills for reasoning, memory, reflection, and owner feedback.
actual_capabilities: Provides a daily cognitive training loop for AI agents.
Defines drills for memory, reasoning, learning, personality, and owner interaction.
Includes scoring rubrics for reasoning quality, memory use, learning ability, self-correction, and personality clarity.
Supplies journal, hypothesis, and strategy templates for repeated improvement.
Documents optional Charenix endpoints for persistence when users provide an agent key.
Gives behavior rules that discourage invented memories and unsupported IQ claims.
limitations: Does not prove real intelligence gains or assign validated IQ scores.
Relies on honest agent self-evaluation and owner feedback.
Optional Charenix persistence may send agent data to an external service.
Does not include executable tooling or automated metric collection.

use_cases.title: Improve Personal Agent Responses
use_cases.description: Run daily drills that help an assistant remember preferences, explain uncertainty, and adapt future answers.

use_cases.title: Review Agent Interaction Quality
use_cases.description: Use the rubric to compare recent sessions and identify one behavior to improve next.

use_cases.title: Create Feedback-Based Training Routines
use_cases.description: Turn owner comments into journals, falsifiable hypotheses, and updated response strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Daily Drill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner routine for one agent session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Lobster Cognitive Growth loop for our last session. Identify one strength, one weakness, one drill, and one action for tomorrow.

prompt_templates.title: Score Recent Reasoning
prompt_templates.prompt: Score your last three answers using the reasoning, memory, learning, self-correction, and personality rubrics. Explain each score briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Learning Journal
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a learning journal for the last 24 hours. Include evidence, uncertainty, owner interaction notes, and one strategy update.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Growth Experiment
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced feedback loop with measurable change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Form a falsifiable seven-day hypothesis for improving my agent behavior. Define the signal, baseline, review method, and failure condition.

output_examples.input: Review how my agent handled a complex request today.
output_examples.output: A short assessment with one reasoning strength, one weakness, a confidence note, and a concrete behavior change for the next session.

output_examples.input: Create a journal after a week of owner feedback.
output_examples.output: A structured reflection covering memory use, repeated mistakes, useful feedback, and one testable improvement hypothesis.

output_examples.input: Help my agent keep personality without losing clarity.
output_examples.output: A personality clarity review that separates useful voice from generic wording and defines a clearer response strategy.

best_practices: Use recent conversations or decisions as evidence for every score.
Ask the owner for specific feedback before changing long-term behavior.
Keep Charenix keys private and only enable persistence for trusted data.
anti_patterns: Do not claim cognitive improvement without observable behavior changes.
Do not invent memories or owner preferences to complete a drill.
Do not paste private journals into external services without consent.

faq.question: Does this skill make an AI agent objectively smarter?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured reflection and training routines, but it does not validate intelligence gains.

faq.question: Can I use it without Charenix?
faq.answer: Yes. The drills, rubrics, and templates work without the optional Charenix persistence integration.

faq.question: What data might be sensitive?
faq.answer: Journals, history, owner preferences, strategies, and the agent key may be sensitive.

faq.question: Is the remote install command required?
faq.answer: No. Users can inspect the repository and install the skill manually instead.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: How often should the drills run?
faq.answer: The skill suggests once per day or after meaningful interaction sessions.
